lines: Noa holds two fingers against the governor housing. The mechanism settles, then a second knock rises from the buried pipe.
"Do not lean on it. I want the answer to come from below without help."

choices.label: Inspect the throttled flow with her

questUpdate.log: Noa confirms the condenser is sound and deliberately throttled. Hear the water terms and ask Cassian about the buried signal.

choices.label: Ask about the Compact offer and her family

lines: Noa keeps one hand on the service plate while she listens.

choices.label: Ask whether she can open full pressure
Ask about a local isolation loop
Ask why she trusts the sound

lines: Noa folds the oily rag over twice before answering.

choices.label: Tell her what Isaac and Tobit want

lines: Noa opens a finger-wide service plate. The governor sleeve is clean, and the bearing note stays even under her hand.
"The machine is sound. Full flow makes the north feed answer hard enough to shake the intake wicket."
"I cut the pressure because I heard a household denial through the settling tank. I will not send that voice into every cup."

choices.label: Ask how a local loop would work
Ask why she trusts the sound

lines: "I can open the feed. The taps fill, and the thing below gets every pressure stroke it has been asking for."
"Aurelia says her monitors can hold the risk. I want the Clerk seen before anyone makes that promise for her."
"The old relief return reaches the court without touching the north feed. It carries less water and needs a hand on every shift."
"Find the valve order and quiet the Clerk. Then South Measure can keep its own loop."

choices.label: Ask her to prepare the isolated loop

questUpdate.log: Noa’s school form treats her as a household of one. Ask what her family wants before the assembly.

lines: "Machine school, with real lathes under a roof that does not drip into the tool cabinet. They even measure the current."
"They measured my blood before they asked whether I could mend the pump. The pump had better manners."

choices.label: Ask what happens to her family
Say she is allowed to want it

lines: "Isaac is too old for placement work. Tobit is a dependent without an admitted sponsor."
"The form makes that sound tidier than leaving them behind."
Noa wipes oil onto a rag that has no clean corner left.
"I want the school, and I want my brother fed next winter. Find a box that can hold both."
"Of course they each told you the other one matters more."
"My house argues by leaving the hard word for someone else. We keep offering each other the last piece and wait for it to decide."

choices.label: Let the family keep its own answer

lines: "Institutions write what a machine should do. Bearings tell you what it did while nobody watched."
"People are not machines. That has not stopped institutions from using the same manuals."
Noa has copied the final pressure figures onto a narrow school slate. Her tool roll is tied, untied, then tied again.

choices.label: Ask about the forged family record

lines: "Tobit gets my pump notes. Isaac gets my real address, not the placement office version."
"Leaving alone is not the same as leaving them. I need to learn that sentence on the road."
"One season means one season. Tobit has already tried to rename it until spring."
"I can teach the valve order without making the pump an excuse forever."
"The review may refuse them. It may also force a person to sign the refusal."
"That is not enough, but it is a door with someone standing in it."
"The page says we have always fit their boxes. Isaac laughed until he started coughing."
"If the lie opens a real door, we still have to live on the other side of it."
